slides.title: The last scroll *costs you sleep*.
slides.sub: Late screens keep your brain alert and push sleep back. A gentle wind-down helps you rest deeper.

slides.title: Light and stimulation delay sleep
slides.body: Bright screens and busy content *can delay* the signals that tell your body it is time to rest.

slides.title: The scroll keeps you alert
slides.body: Each new post nudges your brain awake again. That low buzz *may push back* real sleep.

slides.title: Build a wind-down window
slides.body: Give yourself a quiet half hour before bed. Dim the lights and *let your body settle*.

slides.title: Trade the phone
slides.body: Read a few pages, stretch or simply breathe. A calmer cue *can help* you drift off.

slides.title: Ease into the night
slides.body: You do not need perfect rules. A softer hour before bed *may support* deeper sleep.

slides.title: Wind down for *deeper rest*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your sleep, movement and steps and turns your evenings into one simple daily score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The hour before bed quietly decides how well you sleep. 🌿

Late screens keep your brain alert and push rest back. A gentle wind-down window, with the phone set aside, helps you settle. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your evenings into one simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Winding Down Screens Leads to Deeper Sleep
